5a7f31ecbc68.title: Get your menu online — 77% of diners check before visiting
5a7f31ecbc68.description: No online menu found. Most diners check the menu before committing. Without one, you lose the customer at the decision point.
5a7f31ecbc68.management_implication: Every day without a menu is a percentage of potential customers lost.

12e369a5e396.title: Establish basic web presence — you don't exist online beyond Google
12e369a5e396.description: No website, Facebook, or Instagram detected. Even a basic page improves discoverability and gives customers a reason to trust you before visiting.

0dd6d4d876c4.title: Fix the digital shopfront — you're losing walk-ins
0dd6d4d876c4.description: You deliver a strong experience but your online presence doesn't make it easy to act on. Can a new customer confirm your hours, see your menu, and book — all within 60 seconds on their phone? If not, that's your fix list.
0dd6d4d876c4.management_implication: Interest without conversion is vanity traffic.

91915b9a8047.title: Ranked #4 of 5 locally — peers are ahead
91915b9a8047.description: At percentile 25.0, customers in this area have higher-rated alternatives. This isn't a crisis but it means you're competing on convenience rather than reputation.

0988a8ca08d2.title: Harder to book/find than competitors — friction losing customers
0988a8ca08d2.description: You trail peers by 2.1 points on conversion. This isn't a label problem — it means customers are getting a measurably better experience on this dimension from your competitors.

ad4f15d7e780.title: Accelerate review volume — you're credible but not dominant
ad4f15d7e780.description: At 139 reviews, your rating is stable but not commanding. Venues with 200+ reviews rank higher in Maps. A post-visit review prompt would compound monthly.
